pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states 'Create a VIP Support mailbox'. setup_task.sh lines 22-29 clean up any pre-existing VIP mailbox. export_result.sh line 21 queries for mailbox WHERE LOWER(email)='vip@helpdesk.local' and line 28 reads the name. Metadata value matches task specification exactly.
Task description specifies 'vip@helpdesk.local' as the mailbox email. export_result.sh line 21 uses this exact email in SQL query for verification. Metadata value matches task specification exactly.
setup_task.sh line 15 calls ensure_mailbox_exists with name 'General Support'. Live testing confirmed this mailbox exists and contains 6 seeded conversations.
setup_task.sh line 15 creates mailbox with email 'general@helpdesk.local'. Confirmed via live testing.

Task description states 'Create agent Jordan Mitchell'. export_result.sh line 33 queries for user with email jordan.mitchell@helpdesk.local and reads first_name. Metadata value matches task specification.
Task description states 'Create agent Jordan Mitchell'. export_result.sh line 33 reads last_name from the user record. Metadata value matches task specification.
Task description specifies 'jordan.mitchell@helpdesk.local'. export_result.sh line 33 uses this exact email for lookup. Metadata value matches task specification.
setup_task.sh lines 42-58 create Temp Worker with email 'temp.worker@helpdesk.local', status=1 (Active). Live testing confirmed user exists with this email.

setup_task.sh creates 3 enterprise conversations: Conv1 from infra-ops@pinnacle-systems.com (line 100), Conv2 from accounts-payable@meridian-corp.com (line 138), Conv3 from procurement@summit-engineering.com (line 169). All three domains confirmed via script and live testing.
setup_task.sh creates exactly 3 enterprise conversations: Conv1 (Pinnacle Systems), Conv2 (Meridian Corp), Conv3 (Summit Engineering). Confirmed via live testing — all 3 appeared in General Support.

setup_task.sh creates exactly 3 non-enterprise conversations: Conv4 from sarah.watts@gmail.com, Conv5 from dev.feedback@techstartup.io, Conv6 from jamie.lee@freelance-design.com. Confirmed via live testing.

Task description specifies subject 'VIP Support — Request Received' (with Unicode em dash U+2014). export_result.sh line 112 reads auto_reply_subject from DB for verification. Metadata value matches task specification exactly.

Task description explicitly lists these 3 required phrases for the auto-reply body. export_result.sh line 113 captures auto_reply_message for VLM evaluation. Metadata values match task specification exactly.

pi_items.metadata_value: Synthetic enterprise support scenarios based on realistic corporate helpdesk data
pi_items.verified_value: Fully synthetic — all conversations, customers, and users are generated by setup_task.sh with hardcoded fictional names, emails, and support scenarios. No external dataset is used.
pi_items.source: Analyzed entire setup_task.sh: all customer names (Infrastructure Operations, Accounts Payable, Procurement Department, Sarah Watts, DevOps Team, Jamie Lee), email addresses, and conversation bodies are hardcoded strings. No data downloads, no CSV imports, no external API calls.

privileged_info_summary: This is a fully synthetic multi-step FreeScout helpdesk administration task with no external data sources. The setup creates a 'General Support' mailbox (general@helpdesk.local) containing exactly 6 conversations. Three are from enterprise domains that must be moved to a new VIP Support mailbox: (1) 'Production Server Cluster Failing Health Checks' from infra-ops@pinnacle-systems.com — this conversation is initially CLOSED (status=3) and assigned to Temp Worker, and must be reopened to Active status with an internal note and apology reply; (2) 'Q4 Invoice Discrepancy — Duplicate Charges on Enterprise License' from accounts-payable@meridian-corp.com — Active, unassigned; (3) 'Replacement UPS Battery Units for Server Room B — PO #EQ-5521' from procurement@summit-engineering.com — Active, unassigned. Three non-enterprise conversations must remain in General Support untouched: (4) 'Cannot Reset Password After Email Migration' from sarah.watts@gmail.com (assigned to admin), (5) 'Feature Request — Dark Mode for Dashboard' from dev.feedback@techstartup.io, (6) 'Subscription Billing Date Change Request' from jamie.lee@freelance-design.com. The agent must create a 'VIP Support' mailbox with email vip@helpdesk.local, create user Jordan Mitchell (jordan.mitchell@helpdesk.local) with User role (role=2) and grant access to VIP Support mailbox ONLY. All 3 enterprise conversations must be moved to VIP Support and assigned to Jordan Mitchell. The VIP mailbox auto-reply must have subject 'VIP Support — Request Received' (em dash U+2014) and body containing exact phrases: 'dedicated account manager', '4-hour response guarantee', and 'vip-support@helpdesk.local'. For Conv 1, the agent must reopen it (set status=1), add an internal note (thread type=2) containing 'Reopened per VIP escalation policy — closed in error by previous handler', and send a reply apologizing and confirming Jordan Mitchell as assigned specialist. The Pinnacle Systems customer profile (associated with infra-ops@pinnacle-systems.com) starts with blank Company and Phone — agent must set Company to 'Pinnacle Systems Inc.' and Phone to '+1-415-555-0192'. The Temp Worker account (temp.worker@helpdesk.local, starts status=1 Active) must be deactivated (status=2 or 3) but NOT deleted. FreeScout status codes: 1=Active, 2=Pending, 3=Closed. Thread types: 1=customer message, 2=note. Verification uses phone regex '415.*555.*0192' and case-insensitive grep for 'pinnacle' on company. The verifier.py is a stub; actual verification is done by export_result.sh querying the database.
